name: Artist Relief Fund
description: Emergency grants for artists facing financial crisis due to illness, natural disaster, or other unexpected hardship. Accepts applications on a rolling basis.
eligibility: Working artists of any discipline facing emergency financial need.

name: Foundation for Contemporary Arts — Emergency Grants
description: Emergency assistance for artists whose livelihood has been impacted by unexpected events. Covers visual, performing, and literary artists.
eligibility: Artists with a history of presenting experimental work.

name: PEN America Writers' Emergency Fund
description: Emergency financial assistance for writers in acute financial crisis. Covers fiction, nonfiction, poetry, journalism, and translation.
eligibility: Published writers experiencing sudden financial emergency.

name: Sweet Relief Musicians Fund
description: Financial assistance to career musicians struggling with illness, disability, or age-related problems.
eligibility: Career musicians who have worked professionally for at least five years.

name: The Actors Fund
description: Comprehensive social services and emergency financial assistance for performing arts and entertainment professionals.
eligibility: Anyone who has worked professionally in performing arts or entertainment.

name: Mutual Aid Hub
description: A searchable directory of mutual aid groups across the US. Find local networks offering food, supplies, rent assistance, and community support.
eligibility: Open to all. Search by location to find local groups.

name: COVID-19 Mutual Aid Network — Arts & Culture
description: Peer-to-peer support network for artists and cultural workers offering supplies, childcare, housing leads, and skills exchange.
eligibility: Artists and cultural workers of all disciplines.

name: Trans Lifeline — Microgrants
description: Microgrants for trans people in financial crisis. Covers legal name changes, gender-affirming medical costs, and other emergency needs.

name: Housing Opportunities for People with AIDS (HOPWA)
description: Federal program providing housing assistance and supportive services for low-income people living with HIV/AIDS and their families.

name: National Queer and Trans Therapists of Color Network
description: A healing justice organization committed to transforming mental health access for queer and trans people of color. Therapist directory and mental health fund.
eligibility: QTPOC seeking affirming mental health care. Sliding scale and fund assistance available.

name: Open Path Collective
description: Network of mental health professionals offering sessions between $30–$80 for individuals in financial need.
eligibility: Individuals and families with household income under $100,000.

name: Volunteer Lawyers for the Arts
description: Free and low-cost legal services for artists and arts organizations — contracts, intellectual property, nonprofit formation, and more.
eligibility: Artists and arts organizations who cannot afford private legal counsel.

name: Black Artists + Designers Guild
description: A collective of independent Black artists and designers supporting each other through community, resources, and amplification.

name: Allied Media Projects
description: Fiscal sponsorship and community infrastructure for media-based projects working toward social change.
eligibility: Projects using media and technology for social justice.

name: The Laundromat Project
description: Invests in artists of color to create and teach art within their own communities. Grants, residencies, and community programming.
eligibility: Artists of color working within their own communities.
